NKJV.1: Brethren, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for Israel is that they may be saved.
NKJV.2: For I bear them witness that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge.
NKJV.3: For they being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and seeking to establish their own righteousness, have not submitted to the righteousness of God.
NKJV.4: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
NKJV.5: For Moses writes about the righteousness which is of the law, “The man who does those things shall live by them.”
NKJV.6: But the righteousness of faith speaks in this way, “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’ ” (that is, to bring Christ down from above)
NKJV.7: or, “ ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’ ” (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).
NKJV.8: But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart” (that is, the word of faith which we preach):
NKJV.9: that if you confess with your mouth the Lord Jesus and believe in your heart that God has raised Him from the dead, you will be saved.
NKJV.10: For with the heart one believes unto righteousness, and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.
NKJV.11: For the Scripture says, “Whoever believes on Him will not be put to shame.”
NKJV.12: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek, for the same Lord over all is rich to all who call upon Him.
NKJV.13: For “whoever calls on the name of the LORD shall be saved.”
NKJV.14: How then shall they call on Him in whom they have not believed? And how shall they believe in Him of whom they have not heard? And how shall they hear without a preacher?
NKJV.15: And how shall they preach unless they are sent? As it is written:  “How beautiful are the feet of those who preach the gospel of peace, Who bring glad tidings of good things!”
NKJV.16: But they have not all obeyed the gospel. For Isaiah says, “LORD, who has believed our report?”
NKJV.17: So then faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
NKJV.18: But I say, have they not heard? Yes indeed:  “Their sound has gone out to all the earth, And their words to the ends of the world.”
NKJV.19: But I say, did Israel not know? First Moses says: “I will provoke you to jealousy by those who are not a nation, I will move you to anger by a foolish nation.”
NKJV.20: But Isaiah is very bold and says:  “I was found by those who did not seek Me; I was made manifest to those who did not ask for Me.”
NKJV.21: But to Israel he says:  “All day long I have stretched out My hands To a disobedient and contrary people.”

ESV.1: Brothers,  my heart's desire and prayer to God for them is that they may be saved.
ESV.2: For I bear them witness that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge.
ESV.3: For, being ignorant of the righteousness of God, and seeking to establish their own, they did not submit to God's righteousness.
ESV.4: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
ESV.5: For Moses writes about the righteousness that is based on the law, that the person who does the commandments shall live by them.
ESV.6: But the righteousness based on faith says, “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’” (that is, to bring Christ down)
ESV.7: “or ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’” (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).
ESV.8: But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart” (that is, the word of faith that we proclaim);
ESV.9: because, if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
ESV.10: For with the heart one believes and is justified, and with the mouth one confesses and is saved.
ESV.11: For the Scripture says, “Everyone who believes in him will not be put to shame.”
ESV.12: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; for the same Lord is Lord of all, bestowing his riches on all who call on him.
ESV.13: For “everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.”
ESV.14: How then will they call on him in whom they have not believed? And how are they to believe in him of whom they have never heard?  And how are they to hear without someone preaching?
ESV.15: And how are they to preach unless they are sent? As it is written, “How beautiful are the feet of those who preach the good news!”
ESV.16: But they have not all obeyed the gospel. For Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed what he has heard from us?”
ESV.17: So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ.
ESV.18: But I ask, have they not heard? Indeed they have, for “Their voice has gone out to all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world.”
ESV.19: But I ask, did Israel not understand? First Moses says, “I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation; with a foolish nation I will make you angry.”
ESV.20: Then Isaiah is so bold as to say, “I have been found by those who did not seek me; I have shown myself to those who did not ask for me.”
ESV.21: But of Israel he says, “All day long I have held out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people.”

NASB.1: Brethren, my heart's desire and my prayer to God for them is for [their] salvation.
NASB.2: For I testify about them that they have a zeal for God, but not in accordance with knowledge.
NASB.3: For not knowing about God's righteousness and seeking to establish their own, they did not subject themselves to the righteousness of God.
NASB.4: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
NASB.5: For Moses writes that the man who practices the righteousness which is based on law shall live by that righteousness.
NASB.6: But the righteousness based on faith speaks as follows: 'DO NOT SAY IN YOUR HEART, 'WHO WILL ASCEND INTO HEAVEN?' (that is, to bring Christ down),
NASB.7: or 'WHO WILL DESCEND INTO THE ABYSS?' (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).'
NASB.8: But what does it say? 'THE WORD IS NEAR YOU, IN YOUR MOUTH AND IN YOUR HEART '-- that is, the word of faith which we are preaching,
NASB.9: that if you confess with your mouth Jesus [as] Lord, and believe in your heart that God raised Him from the dead, you will be saved;
NASB.10: for with the heart a person believes, resulting in righteousness, and with the mouth he confesses, resulting in salvation.
NASB.11: For the Scripture says, 'WHOEVER BELIEVES IN HIM WILL NOT BE DISAPPOINTED.'
NASB.12: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; for the same [Lord] is Lord of all, abounding in riches for all who call on Him;
NASB.13: for 'WHOEVER WILL CALL ON THE NAME OF THE LORD WILL BE SAVED.'
NASB.14: How then will they call on Him in whom they have not believed? How will they believe in Him whom they have not heard? And how will they hear without a preacher?
NASB.15: How will they preach unless they are sent? Just as it is written, 'HOW BEAUTIFUL ARE THE FEET OF THOSE WHO BRING GOOD NEWS OF GOOD THINGS!'
NASB.16: However, they did not all heed the good news; for Isaiah says, 'LORD, WHO HAS BELIEVED OUR REPORT?'
NASB.17: So faith [comes] from hearing, and hearing by the word of Christ.
NASB.18: But I say, surely they have never heard, have they? Indeed they have; 'THEIR VOICE HAS GONE OUT INTO ALL THE EARTH, AND THEIR WORDS TO THE ENDS OF THE WORLD.'
NASB.19: But I say, surely Israel did not know, did they? First Moses says, 'I WILL MAKE YOU JEALOUS BY THAT WHICH IS NOT A NATION, BY A NATION WITHOUT UNDERSTANDING WILL I ANGER YOU.'
NASB.20: And Isaiah is very bold and says, 'I WAS FOUND BY THOSE WHO DID NOT SEEK ME, I BECAME MANIFEST TO THOSE WHO DID NOT ASK FOR ME.'
NASB.21: But as for Israel He says, 'ALL THE DAY LONG I HAVE STRETCHED OUT MY HANDS TO A DISOBEDIENT AND OBSTINATE PEOPLE.'

NLT.1: Dear brothers and sisters, the longing of my heart and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
NLT.2: I know what enthusiasm they have for God, but it is misdirected zeal.
NLT.3: For they don’t understand God’s way of making people right with himself. Refusing to accept God’s way, they cling to their own way of getting right with God by trying to keep the law.
NLT.4: For Christ has already accomplished the purpose for which the law was given. As a result, all who believe in him are made right with God.
NLT.5: For Moses writes that the law’s way of making a person right with God requires obedience to all of its commands.
NLT.6: But faith’s way of getting right with God says, “Don’t say in your heart, ‘Who will go up to heaven?’ (to bring Christ down to earth).
NLT.7: And don’t say, ‘Who will go down to the place of the dead?’ (to bring Christ back to life again).”
NLT.8:
In fact, it says, “The message is very close at hand;
it is on your lips and in your heart.”
And that message is the very message about faith that we preach:
NLT.9: If you openly declare that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
NLT.10: For it is by believing in your heart that you are made right with God, and it is by openly declaring your faith that you are saved.
NLT.11: As the Scriptures tell us, “Anyone who trusts in him will never be disgraced.”
NLT.12: Jew and Gentile are the same in this respect. They have the same Lord, who gives generously to all who call on him.
NLT.13: For “Everyone who calls on the name of the LORD will be saved.”
NLT.14: But how can they call on him to save them unless they believe in him? And how can they believe in him if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear about him unless someone tells them?
NLT.15: And how will anyone go and tell them without being sent? That is why the Scriptures say, “How beautiful are the feet of messengers who bring good news!”
NLT.16: But not everyone welcomes the Good News, for Isaiah the prophet said, “LORD, who has believed our message?”
NLT.17: So faith comes from hearing, that is, hearing the Good News about Christ.
NLT.18:
But I ask, have the people of Israel actually heard the message? Yes, they have: “The message has gone throughout the earth,
and the words to all the world.”
NLT.19:
But I ask, did the people of Israel really understand? Yes, they did, for even in the time of Moses, God said, “I will rouse your jealousy through people who are not even a nation.
I will provoke your anger through the foolish Gentiles.”
NLT.20:
And later Isaiah spoke boldly for God, saying, “I was found by people who were not looking for me.
I showed myself to those who were not asking for me.”
NLT.21:
But regarding Israel, God said, “All day long I opened my arms to them,
but they were disobedient and rebellious.”

WEB.1: Brothers, my heart’s desire and my prayer to God is for Israel, that they may be saved.
WEB.2: For I testify about them that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge.
WEB.3: For being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and seeking to establish their own righteousness, they didn’t subject themselves to the righteousness of God.
WEB.4: For Christ is the fulfillment  of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
WEB.5: For Moses writes about the righteousness of the law, “The one who does them will live by them.”
WEB.6: But the righteousness which is of faith says this, “Don’t say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’  (that is, to bring Christ down);
WEB.7: or, ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’  (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead.)”
WEB.8: But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth, and in your heart”;  that is, the word of faith, which we preach:
WEB.9: that if you will confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord, and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
WEB.10: For with the heart, one believes unto righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.
WEB.11: For the Scripture says, “Whoever believes in him will not be disappointed.”
WEB.12: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; for the same Lord is Lord of all, and is rich to all who call on him.
WEB.13: For, “Whoever will call on the name of the Lord will be saved.”
WEB.14: How then will they call on him in whom they have not believed? How will they believe in him whom they have not heard? How will they hear without a preacher?
WEB.15: And how will they preach unless they are sent? As it is written: “How beautiful are the feet of those who preach the Good News of peace, who bring glad tidings of good things!”
WEB.16: But they didn’t all listen to the glad news. For Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed our report?”
WEB.17: So faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
WEB.18: But I say, didn’t they hear? Yes, most certainly, “Their sound went out into all the earth, their words to the ends of the world.”
WEB.19: But I ask, didn’t Israel know? First Moses says, “I will provoke you to jealousy with that which is no nation, with a nation void of understanding I will make you angry.”
WEB.20: Isaiah is very bold, and says, “I was found by those who didn’t seek me. I was revealed to those who didn’t ask for me.”
WEB.21: But as to Israel he says, “All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people.”

CSB17.1: Brothers and sisters, my heart’s desire and prayer to God concerning them <sup>[1]</sup> is for their salvation.
CSB17.2: I can testify about them that they have zeal for God, <sup>ⓑ</sup> but not according to knowledge.
CSB17.3: Since they are ignorant of the righteousness of God <sup>ⓒ</sup> and attempted to establish their own righteousness, they have not submitted to God’s righteousness.
CSB17.4: For Christ is the end <sup>[2]</sup> of the law for righteousness <sup>ⓓ</sup> to everyone who believes, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.5: since Moses writes about the righteousness that is from the law: The one who does these things will live by them. ⓕ ⓖ
CSB17.6: But the righteousness that comes from faith ⓗ speaks like this: Do not say in your heart, “Who will go up to heaven?” ⓘ ⓙ that is, to bring Christ down
CSB17.7: or, “Who will go down into the abyss?” ⓚ ⓛ that is, to bring Christ up from the dead. ⓜ
CSB17.8: On the contrary, what does it say? The message is near you, in your mouth and in your heart. ⓝ ⓐ This is the message of faith that we proclaim:
CSB17.9: If you confess with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” <sup>ⓑ</sup> and believe in your heart <sup>ⓒ</sup> that God raised him from the dead, <sup>ⓓ</sup> you will be saved.
CSB17.10: One believes with the heart, resulting in righteousness, and one confesses with the mouth, resulting in salvation.
CSB17.11: For the Scripture says, Everyone who believes on him will not be put to shame, ⓔ ⓕ
CSB17.12: since there is no distinction between Jew and Greek, <sup>ⓖ</sup> because the same Lord <sup>ⓗ</sup> of all <sup>ⓘ</sup> richly blesses all who call on him.
CSB17.13: For everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. [3] ⓙ
CSB17.14: How, then, can they call on him they have not believed in? And how can they believe without hearing about him? <sup>ⓚ</sup> And how can they hear without a preacher? <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.15: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written: How beautiful [4] are the feet of those who bring good news. [5] ⓜ
CSB17.16: But not all obeyed the gospel. ⓝ For Isaiah says, Lord, who has believed our message? [6] ⓐ
CSB17.17: So faith comes from what is heard, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and what is heard comes through the message about Christ. <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.18:
But I ask, “Did they not hear?” Yes, they did: Their voice has gone out to the whole earth,
and their words to the ends of the world. [8] ⓓ
CSB17.19:
But I ask, “Did Israel not understand?” First, Moses said, I will make you jealous
of those who are not a nation; ⓔ
I will make you angry by a nation
that lacks understanding. [9] ⓕ
CSB17.20:
And Isaiah says boldly, I was found
by those who were not looking for me;
I revealed myself
to those who were not asking for me. [10] ⓖ
CSB17.21: But to Israel he says, All day long I have held out my hands to a disobedient and defiant people. [11] ⓗ

AMP.1: Brothers and sisters, my heart's desire and my prayer to God for Israel is for their salvation.
AMP.2: For I testify about them that they have a certain enthusiasm for God, but not in accordance with [correct and vital] knowledge [about Him and His purposes].
AMP.3: For not knowing about God's righteousness [which is based on faith], and seeking to establish their own [righteousness based on works], they did not submit to God's righteousness.
AMP.4: For Christ is the end of the law [it leads to Him and its purpose is fulfilled in Him], for [granting] righteousness to everyone who believes [in Him as Savior].
AMP.5: For Moses writes that the man who practices the righteousness which is based on law [with all its intricate demands] shall live by it.
AMP.6: But the righteousness based on faith [which produces a right relationship with Him] says the following: "Do not say in your heart, 'Who will ascend into Heaven?' that is, to bring Christ down;
AMP.7: or, 'Who will descend into the abyss?' that is, to bring Christ up from the dead [as if we had to be saved by our own efforts, doing the impossible]."
AMP.8: But what does it say? "The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart"—that is, the word [the message, the basis] of faith which we preach—
AMP.9: because if you acknowledge and confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord [recognizing His power, authority, and majesty as God], and believe in your heart that God raised Him from the dead, you will be saved.
AMP.10: For with the heart a person believes [in Christ as Savior] resulting in his justification [that is, being made righteous—being freed of the guilt of sin and made acceptable to God]; and with the mouth he acknowledges and confesses [his faith openly], resulting in and confirming [his] salvation.
AMP.11: For the Scripture says, "Whoever believes in Him [whoever adheres to, trusts in, and relies on Him] will not be disappointed [in his expectations]."
AMP.12: For there is no distinction between Jew and Gentile; for the same Lord is Lord over all [of us], and [He is] abounding in riches (blessings) for all who call on Him [in faith and prayer].
AMP.13: For "whoever calls on the name of the Lord [in prayer] will be saved."
AMP.14: But how will people call on Him in whom they have not believed? And how will they believe in Him of whom they have not heard? And how will they hear without a preacher (messenger)?
AMP.15: And how will they preach unless they are commissioned and sent [for that purpose]? Just as it is written and forever remains written, "How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news of good things!"
AMP.16: But they did not all pay attention to the good news [of salvation]; for Isaiah says, "Lord, who has believed our report?"
AMP.17: So faith comes from hearing [what is told], and what is heard comes by the [preaching of the] message concerning Christ.
AMP.18:
But I say, did they not hear? Indeed they have;
"Their voice [that of creation bearing God's message] has gone out to all the earth,
And their words to the [farthest] ends of the world."
AMP.19:
But I say, did Israel fail to understand [that the gospel was to go also to the Gentiles]? First Moses says,
"I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation (Gentiles);
With a nation that lacks understanding I will make you angry."
AMP.20:
Then Isaiah is very bold and says,
"I have been found by those who did not seek Me;
I have shown Myself to those who did not [consciously] ask for Me."
AMP.21: But of Israel he says, "All day long I have stretched out My hands [in compassion] to a disobedient and obstinate people."

MSG.1: Believe me, friends, all I want for Israel is what's best for Israel: salvation, nothing less. I want it with all my heart and pray to God for it all the time.
MSG.2: I readily admit that the Jews are impressively energetic regarding God—but they are doing everything exactly backwards.
MSG.3: They don't seem to realize that this comprehensive setting-things-right that is salvation is God's business, and a most flourishing business it is. Right across the street they set up their own salvation shops and noisily hawk their wares. After all these years of refusing to really deal with God on his terms, insisting instead on making their own deals, they have nothing to show for it.
MSG.4: The earlier revelation was intended simply to get us ready for the Messiah, who then puts everything right for those who trust him to do it.
MSG.5: Moses wrote that anyone who insists on using the law code to live right before God soon discovers it's not so easy—every detail of life regulated by fine print!
MSG.6: But trusting God to shape the right living in us is a different story—no precarious climb up to heaven to recruit the Messiah,
MSG.7: no dangerous descent into hell to rescue the Messiah.
MSG.8: So what exactly was Moses saying? The word that saves is right here, as near as the tongue in your mouth, as close as the heart in your chest. It's the word of faith that welcomes God to go to work and set things right for us. This is the core of our preaching.
MSG.9: Say the welcoming word to God—"Jesus is my Master"—embracing, body and soul, God's work of doing in us what he did in raising Jesus from the dead. That's it. You're not "doing" anything; you're simply calling out to God, trusting him to do it for you. That's salvation.
MSG.10: With your whole being you embrace God setting things right, and then you say it, right out loud: "God has set everything right between him and me!"
MSG.11: Scripture reassures us, "No one who trusts God like this—heart and soul—will ever regret it."
MSG.12: It's exactly the same no matter what a person's religious background may be: the same God for all of us, acting the same incredibly generous way to everyone who calls out for help.
MSG.14: But how can people call for help if they don't know who to trust? And how can they know who to trust if they haven't heard of the One who can be trusted? And how can they hear if nobody tells them?
MSG.15: And how is anyone going to tell them, unless someone is sent to do it? That's why Scripture exclaims, A sight to take your breath away! Grand processions of people telling all the good things of God!
MSG.16: But not everybody is ready for this, ready to see and hear and act. Isaiah asked what we all ask at one time or another: "Does anyone care, God? Is anyone listening and believing a word of it?"
MSG.17: The point is, Before you trust, you have to listen. But unless Christ's Word is preached, there's nothing to listen to.
MSG.18: But haven't there been plenty of opportunities for Israel to listen and understand what's going on? Plenty, I'd say. Preachers' voices have gone 'round the world, Their message to earth's seven seas.
MSG.19: So the big question is, Why didn't Israel understand that she had no corner on this message? Moses had it right when he predicted, When you see God reach out to those you consider your inferiors—outsiders!— you'll become insanely jealous. When you see God reach out to people you think are religiously stupid, you'll throw temper tantrums.
MSG.20: Isaiah dared to speak out these words of God: People found and welcomed me who never so much as looked for me. And I found and welcomed people who had never even asked about me.
MSG.21: Then he capped it with a damning indictment: Day after day after day, I beckoned Israel with open arms, And got nothing for my trouble but cold shoulders and icy stares.

NIV.1: Brothers, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for the Israelites is that they may be saved.
NIV.2: For I can testify about them that they are zealous for God, but their zeal is not based on knowledge.
NIV.3: Since they did not know the righteousness that comes from God and sought to establish their own, they did not submit to God’s righteousness.
NIV.4: Christ is the end of the law so that there may be righteousness for everyone who believes.
NIV.5: Moses describes in this way the righteousness that is by the law: “The man who does these things will live by them.”
NIV.6: But the righteousness that is by faith says: “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’” (that is, to bring Christ down)
NIV.7: “or ‘Who will descend into the deep?’” (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).
NIV.8: But what does it say? “The word is near you; it is in your mouth and in your heart,” that is, the word of faith we are proclaiming:
NIV.9: That if you confess with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
NIV.10: For it is with your heart that you believe and are justified, and it is with your mouth that you confess and are saved.
NIV.11: As the Scripture says, “Anyone who trusts in him will never be put to shame.”
NIV.12: For there is no difference between Jew and Gentile—the same Lord is Lord of all and richly blesses all who call on him,
NIV.13: for, “Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.”
NIV.14: How, then, can they call on the one they have not believed in? And how can they believe in the one of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone preaching to them?
NIV.15: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, “How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!”
NIV.16: But not all the Israelites accepted the good news. For Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed our message?”
NIV.17: Consequently, faith comes from hearing the message, and the message is heard through the word of Christ.
NIV.18:
But I ask: Did they not hear? Of course they did:
“Their voice has gone out into all the earth,
their words to the ends of the world.”
NIV.19:
Again I ask: Did Israel not understand? First, Moses says,
“I will make you envious by those who are not a nation;
I will make you angry by a nation that has no understanding.”
NIV.20:
And Isaiah boldly says,
“I was found by those who did not seek me;
I revealed myself to those who did not ask for me.”
NIV.21:
But concerning Israel he says,
“All day long I have held out my hands
to a disobedient and obstinate people.”

NRSVCE.1: Brothers and sisters, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for them is that they may be saved.
NRSVCE.2: I can testify that they have a zeal for God, but it is not enlightened.
NRSVCE.3: For, being ignorant of the righteousness that comes from God, and seeking to establish their own, they have not submitted to God’s righteousness.
NRSVCE.4: For Christ is the end of the law so that there may be righteousness for everyone who believes.
NRSVCE.5: Moses writes concerning the righteousness that comes from the law, that “the person who does these things will live by them.”
NRSVCE.6: But the righteousness that comes from faith says, “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’” (that is, to bring Christ down)
NRSVCE.7: “or ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’” (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).
NRSVCE.8:
But what does it say? “The word is near you,
on your lips and in your heart”
(that is, the word of faith that we proclaim);
NRSVCE.9: because if you confess with your lips that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
NRSVCE.10: For one believes with the heart and so is justified, and one confesses with the mouth and so is saved.
NRSVCE.11: The scripture says, “No one who believes in him will be put to shame.”
NRSVCE.12: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; the same Lord is Lord of all and is generous to all who call on him.
NRSVCE.13: For, “Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord shall be saved.”
NRSVCE.14: But how are they to call on one in whom they have not believed? And how are they to believe in one of whom they have never heard? And how are they to hear without someone to proclaim him?
NRSVCE.15: And how are they to proclaim him unless they are sent? As it is written, “How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!”
NRSVCE.16: But not all have obeyed the good news; for Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed our message?”
NRSVCE.17: So faith comes from what is heard, and what is heard comes through the word of Christ.
NRSVCE.18:
But I ask, have they not heard? Indeed they have; for “Their voice has gone out to all the earth,
and their words to the ends of the world.”
NRSVCE.19:
Again I ask, did Israel not understand? First Moses says, “I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation;
with a foolish nation I will make you angry.”
NRSVCE.20:
Then Isaiah is so bold as to say, “I have been found by those who did not seek me;
I have shown myself to those who did not ask for me.”
NRSVCE.21: But of Israel he says, “All day long I have held out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people.”

NET.1: Brothers and sisters, my heart’s desire and prayer to God on behalf of my fellow Israelites is for their salvation.
NET.2: For I can testify that they are zealous for God, but their zeal is not in line with the truth.
NET.3: For ignoring the righteousness that comes from God, and seeking instead to establish their own righteousness, they did not submit to God’s righteousness.
NET.4: For Christ is the end of the law, with the result that there is righteousness for everyone who believes.
NET.5: For Moses writes about the righteousness that is by the law: “The one who does these things will live by them.”
NET.6: But the righteousness that is by faith says: “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’” (that is, to bring Christ down)
NET.7: or “Who will descend into the abyss?” (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).
NET.8: But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart” (that is, the word of faith that we preach),
NET.9: because if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
NET.10: For with the heart one believes and thus has righteousness and with the mouth one confesses and thus has salvation.
NET.11: For the scripture says, “Everyone who believes in him will not be put to shame.”
NET.12: For there is no distinction between the Jew and the Greek, for the same Lord is Lord of all, who richly blesses all who call on him.
NET.13: For everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.
NET.14: How are they to call on one they have not believed in? And how are they to believe in one they have not heard of? And how are they to hear without someone preaching to them ?
NET.15: And how are they to preach unless they are sent? As it is written, “How timely is the arrival of those who proclaim the good news.”
NET.16: But not all have obeyed the good news, for Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed our report?”
NET.17: Consequently faith comes from what is heard, and what is heard comes through the preached word of Christ.
NET.18: But I ask, have they not heard? Yes, they have: Their voice has gone out to all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world.
NET.19: But again I ask, didn’t Israel understand? First Moses says, “I will make you jealous by those who are not a nation; with a senseless nation I will provoke you to anger.”
NET.20: And Isaiah is even bold enough to say, “I was found by those who did not seek me; I became well known to those who did not ask for me.”
NET.21: But about Israel he says, “All day long I held out my hands to this disobedient and stubborn people!”
